original_abstract: This inquiry explores the critical tension between hope as a transformative antidote to despair and hope as a potential form of escapism from the realities of suffering. Central to this discussion is the nature of hope within Christian spirituality and its theological grounding in the resurrection. Far from being a expression of "cheap triumphalism" or a superficial theology of glory, a *theologia resurrectionis* provides a radical reframing of existence through the paradox of victory over death. In the context of pastoral care, the resurrection establishes an ontology of hope that facilitates a profound transformation of the human person. By moving beyond the stigmatizing social and cultural categories of race, gender, and status, resurrection theology asserts that human identity is defined by eschatology and the promise of a "new creation." Ultimately, "hope care" fosters a renewed courage to be, offering alternative frameworks for meaningful living amidst the persistence of human anguish.
humanized_abstract: Hope as Restorative for Human Suffering? or as a Refuge from it? This consultation will try to establish how the resource of hope might be understood in relation to its potential for transforming suffering on the one hand and evacuating us from its presence on the other. Of particular concern will be the form of Christian hope understood in relation to the resurrection of the Lord, which we describe as the theologia resurrectionis—glory without becoming cheap triumphalism, glory that is more than the theology of glory! By bringing all things under the resurrection we have a whole new ontology for hope as a kind of dynamic re-presentation of the human person in the world for care. Bringing hope beyond the borders of ethnicity, sex, social status, the resurrection affirms human identity as essentially eschatological—the humanity as * creatura* within the promise of the new creation. Hope care as care for human being thereby fosters new forms of being in the world that empowers us with a fortitude for living life with hardship in between.
